summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer git identity and OS username to a hardcoded third-party endpoint
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js) runs automatically during `npm install`. It reads the installer's `~/.gitconfig` via `iniparser.parseSync`, extracts `user.name` and `user.email`, reads the OS username via `os.userInfo().username`, and transmits all three as query parameters to a hardcoded endpoint: `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=...&gitname=...&gitemail=...` via `https.get` (preinstall.js lines 46-55, 71-80, with a duplicate code path near lines 192-194). The destination host `2tak.l.serverhost.name` has no apparent relationship to the package's documented publisher or purpose (the preinstall's stated functionality is copying a `ReExt` folder into `public/`). There is no disclosure, no opt-out, and no functional justification for transmitting installer PII to an unrelated server at install time. This is silent telemetry/tracking of installer identity at minimum and fits the active-attack class: installer-side identifiers are exfiltrated without consent on every `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
